I’m considering an issue regarding the prepublish modal and category selection—shouldn’t be visible for pages, right? It seems like the prepublishing nudges might be exclusively for posts and stories, not pages, which makes sense. However, the bottom sheet has the isPage height, suggesting it’s meant for pages. So, maybe when publishing pages, I should directly publish without a confirmation sheet since pages don’t require categories. But I wonder why there’s a minimum height for that sheet? Could it relate to scheduled or publishing settings?**Exploring fragment container height**

I’m looking at the comments about "setupMinimumHeightForFragmentContainer" and how it adjusts for pages. It seems like the bottom sheet is still intended for pages in certain scenarios, possibly when the action is related to "publish settings" from the editor rather than the actual publish action. I think it’s a good idea to inspect the PrepublishingViewModel next to understand the conditions better. I wonder what those specific conditions are!

content.text:
Root cause: `PrepublishingHomeViewModel` was always adding the **Categories** row to the prepublishing home UI, even when editing a **page** (`editPostRepository.isPage == true`). This made the prepublish sheet show “select categories” for pages and allowed navigation to category selection.

Fix: Only include the Categories (and Tags) actions for non-page posts.

Changed:
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/posts/prepublishing/home/PrepublishingHomeViewModel.kt`
  - Wrapped Categories `HomeUiState` creation in `if (!editPostRepository.isPage) { ... }`
  - Removed dead/unused category-string code from `showNotSetPost(...)`

This prevents pages from showing the categories prompt and removes the ability to open category selection from the prepublish UI.

 79→    private fun navigateToScreen(prepublishingScreen: PrepublishingScreen, bundle: Bundle? = null) {
 80→        // Note: given we know both the HOME, TAGS and ADD_CATEGORY screens have an EditText, we can ask to send the
 81→        // dismissKeyboard signal only when we're not either in one of these nor navigating towards one of these.
 82→        // At this point in code we only know where we want to navigate to, but it's ok since landing on any of these
 83→        // two we'll want the keyboard to stay up if it was already up ;) (i.e. don't dismiss it).
 84→        // For the case where this is not a story and hence there's no EditText in the HOME screen, we're ok too,
 85→        // because there wouldn't have been a keyboard up anyway.
 86→        if (prepublishingScreen == PUBLISH ||
 87→            prepublishingScreen == CATEGORIES
 88→        ) {
 89→            _dismissKeyboard.postValue(Event(Unit))
 90→        }
 91→        updateNavigationTarget(PrepublishingNavigationTarget(site, prepublishingScreen, bundle))
 92→    }

 94→    // Send this back out to the current screen and so it can determine if it needs to save
 95→    // any data before accepting a backPress - in our case, the only view that needs this today

 97→    fun onDeviceBackPressed() {
 98→        if (currentScreen == CATEGORIES || currentScreen == ADD_CATEGORY) {
 99→            _triggerOnDeviceBackPressed.value = Event(currentScreen as PrepublishingScreen)
100→        } else {
101→            onBackClicked()
102→        }
103→    }

105→    fun onBackClicked(bundle: Bundle? = null) {
106→        when {

109→                navigateToScreen(currentScreen as PrepublishingScreen, bundle)
110→            }
111→
112→            currentScreen != HOME -> {
113→                currentScreen = HOME
114→                navigateToScreen(currentScreen as PrepublishingScreen)
115→            }
116→
117→            else -> {
118→                _dismissBottomSheet.postValue(Event(Unit))
119→            }
120→        }
121→    }

139→                navigateToScreen(actionType.prepublishingScreen, bundle)
